**Unión Magdalena** (Spanish pronunciation: [[uˈnjom maɣðaˈlena]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) commonly known as **El Unión**, and nicknamed as **El Ciclón Bananero** (The Banana Cyclone) and also **Los Samarios** (The Samarios), is a Colombian football club from the city of [Santa Marta](/wiki/Santa_Marta "Santa Marta"), capital of the department of [Magdalena](/wiki/Magdalena_Department "Magdalena Department"), founded on April 19 1953. They compete in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"), the first division of the [Colombian football league system](/wiki/Colombian_football_league_system "Colombian football league system").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The club's home ground is the [Estadio Sierra Nevada](/wiki/Estadio_Sierra_Nevada "Estadio Sierra Nevada"), since 2018.

Founded on 10 March 1951 as ***Deportivo Samarios***, the club was re-founded as Unión Magdalena on 19 April 1953. The original playing squad was largely composed of Hungarians who were touring Colombia when their club, the [Hungaria FbC Roma](/wiki/Hungaria_FbC_Roma "Hungaria FbC Roma"), was disbanded.[[2]](#cite_note-ElTiempo1-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Uni%C3%B3n_Magdalena&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Unión Magdalena was founded as *Deportivo Samarios* on 10 March 1951. The team was founded with the help of José Eduardo Gnecco Correa and Eduardo Dávila Riascos. It was formed by the [Hungaria FbC Roma](/wiki/Hungaria_FbC_Roma "Hungaria FbC Roma") squad when it was disbanded after a tour in South America.[[2]](#cite_note-ElTiempo1-2) The first team consisted of 10 Colombians, 8 Hungarians, 2 Yugoslavs, 1 Austrian, 1 Argentine, 1 Italian and 1 Romanian. Deportivo Samarios debuted in the league that same year and placed 14th. That season, the team achieved the record of the biggest win in the Colombian league against [Universidad](/wiki/National_University_of_Colombia "National University of Colombia"), scoring 12–1.

The club was re-founded as Unión Magdalena on 19 April 1953. Unión has only won one championship, in [1968](/wiki/1968_Campeonato_Profesional "1968 Campeonato Profesional") playing against [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali") in the final, thus becoming the first football club of the [Caribbean region of Colombia](/wiki/Caribbean_region_of_Colombia "Caribbean region of Colombia") to win a football championship in history. [Carlos Valderrama](/wiki/Carlos_Valderrama_(footballer) "Carlos Valderrama (footballer)"), born in Santa Marta and one of the most famous Colombian footballer, started his career in this club.

Relegated in 2005 after losing 3–0 to [Deportivo Pereira](/wiki/Deportivo_Pereira "Deportivo Pereira"), Unión earned automatic promotion to the [2019 Categoría Primera A season](/wiki/2019_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2019 Categoría Primera A season"), after playing 13 years in the second division football league.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Despite qualifying for the semifinals of the 2019 Apertura, Unión's performance in the Finalización tournament was poor and the club ended up being relegated on 29 October after losing 3–1 to [Once Caldas](/wiki/Once_Caldas "Once Caldas").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Red and blue stripes compose the traditional shirt of the team, and its design is inspired by the Argentine club [San Lorenzo de Almagro](/wiki/San_Lorenzo_de_Almagro "San Lorenzo de Almagro").

### 2021 controversy and promotion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Uni%C3%B3n_Magdalena&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 2021 controversy and promotion")]

On 4 December 2021, the last matchday of the semi-final Group B of the second tournament of the [2021 Primera B season](/wiki/2021_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B_season "2021 Categoría Primera B season"), Unión Magdalena sealed their promotion to [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A") after coming back from a 1–0 deficit against [Llaneros](/wiki/Llaneros_F.C. "Llaneros F.C.") in [Villavicencio](/wiki/Villavicencio "Villavicencio") with a couple of goals in quick succession in stoppage time to win the game by a 2–1 score and get promoted at the expense of [Fortaleza](/wiki/Fortaleza_C.E.I.F. "Fortaleza C.E.I.F."), who were clinching promotion with the score in Villavicencio despite losing their final match to [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1_F.C. "Bogotá F.C.") at the same time. However, video footage of Unión Magdalena's winning goal appeared to show the Llaneros players backing off and failing to make an effort to prevent their rivals from scoring.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The events sparked outrage both within the country and abroad, with [Colombian](/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team "Colombia national football team") internationals [Juan Cuadrado](/wiki/Juan_Cuadrado "Juan Cuadrado") and [Mateus Uribe](/wiki/Mateus_Uribe "Mateus Uribe") expressing their displeasure, calling them "a lack of respect" and "an embarrassment for Colombian football", whilst President of Colombia [Iván Duque Márquez](/wiki/Iv%C3%A1n_Duque_M%C3%A1rquez "Iván Duque Márquez") stated it was a "national disgrace".[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In response to the growing backlash, as well as calls from Fortaleza to get the match annulled and Unión Magdalena's promotion reversed,[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Dimayor](/wiki/Divisi%C3%B3n_Mayor_del_F%C3%BAtbol_Profesional_Colombiano "División Mayor del Fútbol Profesional Colombiano") chairman Fernando Jaramillo ordered the opening of an inquiry on the match events while also requesting the [Office of the Attorney General of Colombia](/wiki/Office_of_the_Attorney_General_of_Colombia "Office of the Attorney General of Colombia") to investigate whether any criminal offenses had been committed,[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) but on 7 December 2021, Jaramillo stated that the tournament would not be paused and Unión Magdalena's promotion would not be overturned while due process was completed.[[12]](#cite_note-12) However, amid the ongoing inquiries, the Torneo II's final match between [Cortuluá](/wiki/Cortulu%C3%A1 "Cortuluá") and Unión Magdalena, which was originally scheduled to be played on 11 December 2021 in [Tuluá](/wiki/Tulu%C3%A1 "Tuluá") was postponed until further notice.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

On 30 December 2021, Dimayor's disciplinary commission closed the investigation on Unión Magdalena as it found no evidence implying that members of the aforementioned club had been responsible for the events that occurred in the match against Llaneros, thus confirming their promotion to Primera A for the [2022 season](/wiki/2022_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2022 Categoría Primera A season").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Stadiums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Uni%C3%B3n_Magdalena&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Stadiums")]

The Eduardo Santos Stadium, inaugurated in 1951, was the highest sports venue for [Magdalenes](/wiki/Magdalena_Department "Magdalena Department"), located in the Olympic Village of Santa Marta. It served for the Unión Magdalena local games but since its inauguration, it has not only been the headquarters of the Unión Magdalena soccer club, it also witnessed the birth of prominent Colombian soccer figures such as the former Colombian team captain, [Carlos "El Pibe" Valderrama](/wiki/Carlos_Valderrama "Carlos Valderrama"). It currently has a capacity of 23,000 spectators.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On 3 March 2013, Unión Magdalena played its last game at the stadium against [Llaneros F.C.](/wiki/Llaneros_F.C. "Llaneros F.C."), in compliance with the final closure order of the Eduardo Santos Stadium. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The team had a tour of venues around Riohacha (La Guajira) at the Federico Serrano Soto Stadium and then in the Magdalena municipality of Ciénaga, at the "Luis Tete Samper" Municipal Stadium, with a capacity of 5,000 spectators.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

After the inauguration of the Sierra Nevada Stadium for the 2017 Bolivarian Games, the Union Magdalena agreed with the mayor of Santa Marta official to return to the city from the [2018 season](/wiki/2018_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B_season "2018 Categoría Primera B season").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
